question.content: From the hill-and-train geometry in the figure, compute the critical minimum speed required by the stated condition. Output exactly one decimal number with 1 decimal place, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: a decimal number rounded to one decimal place; write only the numerical value itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Average-height / area method: treat the train as a uniform line distribution and compute the energy rise from the area of the hill profile covered by the train, instead of locating the center of mass directly.
step_1.key_point: Potential-energy increase equals weight times the average vertical rise of the whole train.

step_3.content: First read the hill geometry. The triangular profile has base l and height h, with left and right side lengths l1 and l2. These geometric data determine the total raised area of the profile.
step_3.key_point: The hill is handled as a geometric area, not as a point-mass height problem.

step_5.content: At the critical instant, the full hill segment of length l1+l2 lies under the train. For a uniform train, the total gravitational rise is proportional to the triangular area under that segment.
step_5.key_point: The raised mass fraction is converted into area under the height curve along the train length.

step_7.content: Replace the same triangular area by an equivalent rectangle of width L and average height y. Then Ly equals the hill area, so y is the average rise of the whole train. The minimum speed follows from equating the initial kinetic energy to Mg times this average rise.
step_7.key_point: This route uses average height from area equivalence rather than the h/2 center-of-mass statement of solution1.

step_9.content: Using the geometric data gives h=48, then the average rise is y=h(l1+l2)/(2L). Substituting into the energy threshold yields the required critical minimum speed, rounded to one decimal place.
step_9.key_point: After substitution, the numerical result is 11.5.
